title: PgBouncer
description: PgBouncer connection pooler health: client/server pool states, query and transaction throughput, wait times, network, prepared statements, and per-database connections. Built on the prometheus-community pgbouncer_exporter metrics.

var_service_name.name: service_name
var_service_name.description: Filter by PgBouncer instance (service.name resource attribute).

var_database.name: database
var_database.description: Filter per-database panels by pooled database (database label).

widgets.title: PgBouncer Up
widgets.description: Exporter scrape status. 1 means PgBouncer is reachable and responding.

widgets.title: Queries /s
widgets.description: SQL queries pooled per second across all databases.

widgets.title: Transactions /s
widgets.description: SQL transactions pooled per second across all databases.

widgets.title: Avg Query Duration
widgets.description: Mean query execution time from rate(queries_duration_seconds) / rate(queries_pooled).

widgets.title: Max Client Wait
widgets.description: Age of the oldest unserved client connection. Non-zero means clients are queuing for a server connection.

widgets.title: Active Pools
widgets.description: Number of distinct (database, user) connection pools.

widgets.title: Client Connections (pooler-wide)
widgets.description: Free, used, and login client connection slots across the whole pooler.

widgets.title: Server Connections (pooler-wide)
widgets.description: Free and used server connection slots to PostgreSQL across the whole pooler.

widgets.title: Client Connections by State
widgets.description: Client connections grouped by connection state (active, waiting, idle). The live mix depends on pool mode and load.

widgets.title: Client Connections by Database
widgets.description: Active client connections grouped by pooled database.

widgets.title: Pool Client Active Connections by Database
widgets.description: Client connections linked to a server and able to process queries, per database.

widgets.title: Pool Client Waiting Connections by Database
widgets.description: Client connections waiting for a server connection, per database. Sustained values above zero mean the pool is too small for demand.

widgets.title: Server Connection States
widgets.description: Server connections by state across all pools: active (serving a client), idle (ready), used (idle past server_check_delay), testing, and logging in.

widgets.title: Client Max Wait by Database
widgets.description: Age of the oldest unserved client connection per database. The single most important saturation signal — should normally be zero.

widgets.title: Queries /s by Database
widgets.description: SQL queries pooled per second, per database.

widgets.title: Transactions /s by Database
widgets.description: SQL transactions pooled per second, per database.

widgets.title: Avg Query Duration
widgets.description: Mean query execution time from rate(queries_duration_seconds) / rate(queries_pooled), across all databases.

widgets.title: Avg Transaction Time
widgets.description: Mean time PgBouncer holds a server connection per transaction, from rate(server_in_transaction_seconds) / rate(sql_transactions_pooled). High values keep server connections busy and reduce pool availability.

widgets.title: Client Wait Time /s by Database
widgets.description: Wait-seconds accrued by clients per second (rate of the cumulative client_wait_seconds counter), per database. Above zero means clients are queuing for a server connection; cross-check with Client Max Wait.

widgets.title: Network Throughput
widgets.description: Bytes per second received from and sent to clients across all databases.

widgets.title: Server Assignments /s
widgets.description: Rate at which client connections are assigned a server connection. Indicates pool churn.

widgets.title: Prepared Statement Ops /s
widgets.description: Rate of prepared-statement protocol messages: client Parse, server Parse (forwarded to PostgreSQL), and Bind.

widgets.title: Per-Database Connections
widgets.description: Current connections, configured pool size, and connection limit per database.

widgets.title: Database Connections vs Pool Size
widgets.description: Current backend connections compared with the configured pool size, per database. Current approaching pool size means the pool is fully utilized.

widgets.title: DNS Cache
widgets.description: Cached DNS names and zones, plus in-flight DNS lookups. Spikes in in-flight queries can delay new server connections.

widgets.title: Database Paused / Disabled State
widgets.description: Per-database paused and disabled flags (1 = paused/disabled). Any non-zero value means clients to that database are being held or rejected.

widgets.title: Max Client Conn (config)
widgets.description: Configured max_client_conn — the ceiling on total client connections PgBouncer will accept.

widgets.title: Max User Conn (config)
widgets.description: Configured max_user_connections — the per-user server connection ceiling (0 = unlimited).
